turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: Hi, thanks for seeing me. I've been having some really bad abdominal pain and diarrhea for the past three weeks - it's been getting worse over time.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: It's been affecting my work performance, too, which is making things even more stressful. I've tried to just power through with loperamide, but it's not really helping and I'm getting worried that something serious is going on.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: Besides the pain and diarrhea, I've also been feeling really fatigued - it's like I just can't seem to catch a break from being tired.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: No, I don't usually feel that way. I get hot and sweaty sometimes when I'm stressed, like after work.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The pain is mostly on my left side, but sometimes it can radiate to my lower back and even feel a bit stiff in general. But the abdominal pain is definitely where I feel it most.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: The joint pain has been bothering me too, especially in my knees and ankles - it's more of a dull ache that comes and goes.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: The joint pain is actually pretty consistent, but no swelling or redness at all. It's just this achy feeling that's been persistent for a while now.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: Sometimes I feel nauseous or have stomach upset in the morning after waking up with diarrhea.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy levels have been really low and my appetite has actually decreased significantly over the past few weeks - it's like I've lost my taste for food altogether.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: No, I haven't had any fevers or night sweats that I can think of. But I have noticed a low-grade fever a few times a week, usually around 37.8-38.0°C.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I've had an appendectomy at age 16, that's about it.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: I've been trying to manage my symptoms on my own and haven't seen a doctor in over 2 years, so I'm not really sure what's going on. But I have been taking ibuprofen for joint pain, which seems to make my abdominal symptoms worse.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: I've been having a tough time lately with my symptoms and work schedule, so I've mostly just been grabbing takeout or eating at restaurants because it's easier than cooking at home.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: To be honest, exercising has fallen by the wayside lately. I used to try to stay active, but my symptoms have been making it really hard for me to find the energy or motivation to do that anymore. I've just been trying to get through each day as best I can.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30 years old. That's been tough for our whole family.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: It's definitely crossed my mind, especially since my symptoms started getting worse and I've been trying to figure out what's going on. But I don't want to jump to conclusions or think that I have something serious just because it runs in my family.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I drink a couple of beers most weeknights, but lately, I've actually increased that to 4-5 per night because my symptoms and work have been stressing me out so much.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: I'm a non-smoker and I don't use any recreational drugs. Just coffee, beer, and the occasional glass of wine on the weekends when I can manage it. But like I said, my drinking has definitely increased lately due to stress.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: I've been feeling really anxious about the possibility of something serious being wrong with me. The weight loss and fatigue are especially concerning for me because they're affecting my work and daily life.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: I'm just really scared that I might have something serious going on. My sister's diagnosis was a huge wake-up call for me, and now that my symptoms are worsening, it's hard not to think about the worst-case scenario.
